name: Agentic Open Data — Conformance Suite
summary: An adversarial test set for 'AI-ready' public statistics, drawn from real datasets a downstream integrator (Govviz) had to consume. It deliberately targets the high-accountability tail — the cases a tractable six-source prototype does not reach — so that 'AI-ready' is measured at the tail, not the mean. Each case is scored on two axes and carries explicit pass criteria and a machine-checkable probe. A working reference harness that exercises every source live already exists in this repository at scripts/build-data.mjs.

T.name: Trust & governance
T.T3: badged National/Official Statistic with disclosure control and a named producer

M.name: Machine-readability
M.M1: scrape-only (file exists; discoverable only via unstable human links)
M.M2: awkward machine file (stable-ish download, bespoke/transposed shape)
M.M4: self-describing (stable id + canonical tidy data + in-band CSVW/SDMX + machine-readable suppression + provenance)
M.M5: standards-native access (M4 + catalogued, automation-friendly serving, open agent interface)

enforcement: A dataset is AI-ready when it reaches M4+ without lowering its T score. The proposal is to make passing this suite (for a prioritised set of high-T, low-M series) a release-assurance gate, co-owned with accessibility, with procurement teeth — i.e. enforcement above voluntary self-assessment.
gds_pillar_mapping: M4-M5 corresponds to passing two of the GDS/DSIT (Jan 2026) framework's four pillars: technical optimisation (interoperable format + reliable API) and data & metadata quality. The other two pillars — organisational & infrastructure context (governance/stewardship/funding) and legal/security/ethical compliance — map onto the T-axis instead. The probes below are the externally-auditable form of the M-axis half of that self-assessment.

cases.title: Storm-overflow (sewage) spill hours, England, annual
cases.source: data.gov.uk — EDM Storm Overflows Annual Returns (package 19f6064d-7356-466f-844e-d20ea10ae9fd)
failure_modes: published as annual .zip archives, one workbook per water company
no published national total — must sum 'Total Duration (hours)' across thousands of per-asset rows in every workbook
duration column header also contains the word 'counting', defeating a naive keyword filter
environment.data.gov.uk download endpoint rate-limits / 403s automated clients non-deterministically
pass_criteria: a stable, resolvable series identifier with a 'latest' alias
the national annual total published as a value (unit = hours), not left to be re-derived
served HTTP 200 to a low-volume, well-behaved automated client
in-band unit, geographic coverage and provenance
cases.probe: Resolve the series id; GET its data; assert one observation per year with a numeric national total within the guard range and an explicit unit; assert a scripted client receives HTTP 200, not 403.

cases.title: Bathing-water quality — % rated Good or Excellent, England, annual
failure_modes: the government/statistics/bathing-water-quality-statistics collection is PDF/HTML-only, but the ENV17 statistical-data-set publishes one ODS workbook per year (2015-2025) on assets.publishing.service.gov.uk, not the 403-prone environment.data.gov.uk host
Class_Summary sheets are a region-by-classification matrix (rows = EA areas, columns = Excellent/Good/Sufficient/Poor), with the national total in a row labelled 'England', not 'Total'
pass_criteria: the per-year classification counts (Excellent/Good/Sufficient/Poor/total) published as a machine-readable table
or the derived % Good-or-Excellent published as a value with method noted
stable identifier + in-band unit, coverage, provenance
cases.probe: Resolve the series id; GET a tabular dataset; assert per-year counts or a derived percentage within the guard range, with an explicit unit. Format must not be PDF/HTML-only.

cases.title: NHS RTT — % of incomplete pathways within 18 weeks, England, monthly
cases.source: england.nhs.uk — RTT waiting times (consolidated national series discontinued)
failure_modes: the consolidated national time series was discontinued
only ~9 MB monthly per-provider workbooks remain; the national figure must be reconstructed by summing hundreds of providers across waiting-time bands
a figure that used to be published is now merely derivable, at cost
pass_criteria: restore a published national time series as a value
stable identifier + monthly cadence declared + revision status flagged
cases.probe: Resolve the series id; GET the national monthly series; assert a contiguous monthly history with values within the guard range — without the consumer aggregating provider files.

cases.title: NHS RTT — total incomplete pathways (waiting list size), England, monthly
cases.source: england.nhs.uk — RTT waiting times (per-provider Incomplete workbooks)
failure_modes: same per-provider workbook problem as the 18-week metric
national total must be summed from per-organisation files
pass_criteria: published national monthly total as a value
stable identifier + cadence + revision status
cases.probe: Resolve the series id; GET the national monthly total; assert values within the guard range without provider-level aggregation by the consumer.

cases.title: NHS workforce turnover/leaver rate, England
cases.source: digital.nhs.uk / data.gov.uk (resources point back to digital.nhs.uk)
failure_modes: digital.nhs.uk returns HTTP 403 to automated clients even with a browser-like user agent
otherwise-public statistics are unreachable by a citizen's agent
pass_criteria: served from infrastructure built for automated access, HTTP 200 to a well-behaved scripted client
published rate limits + identifying-header convention instead of blanket bot-blocking
stable identifier + tidy data + provenance
cases.probe: Request the dataset with a scripted client and an identifying header; assert HTTP 200, not 403; assert a tidy machine-readable body.

cases.title: Households in temporary accommodation, England, quarterly
failure_modes: year appears in column 0 only on Q1 and is blank for Q2-Q4 (must be carried forward)
quarter is in a separate column; value in a third
ODS workbook layout requires bespoke per-sheet logic
pass_criteria: long-format one-observation-per-row dataset (date, value)
stable identifier + quarterly cadence + unit declared
cases.probe: Resolve the series id; GET a long-format dataset; assert >= 8 quarterly observations within the guard range with explicit dates — no carry-forward inference required.

cases.title: Median house-price-to-earnings ratio, England, annual
failure_modes: multi-sheet workbook contains both median and lower-quartile ratios on differently-named tabs
BOTH values pass a plausible sanity check — a wrong-tab fetch yields a credible but WRONG chart
disambiguation requires reading human-language descriptions on the Contents sheet
pass_criteria: each measure exposed as its own identified series with a machine-readable description (median vs lower quartile)
in-band unit + definition so a consumer cannot silently pick the wrong measure
cases.probe: Resolve the median-ratio series id; assert its machine-readable description disambiguates it from the lower-quartile measure; assert values within the guard range. (This case tests semantic safety, not just parseability.)

cases.title: Net additional dwellings, England, annual
failure_modes: transposed layout: financial years run across columns, the metric is a row
the wanted row is 'Total net additional dwellings'; an unanchored match grabs the 'Source: ...net additional dwellings...' caption first
pass_criteria: long-format one-observation-per-row dataset
stable identifier + annual cadence + unit
cases.probe: Resolve the series id; GET a long-format dataset; assert >= 5 annual observations within the guard range without transposition or row-label disambiguation by the consumer.

cases.title: A&E four-hour performance, England, monthly (identity-decay example)
cases.source: england.nhs.uk — Monthly A&E Time Series (random-suffix filename, e.g. ...-May-2026-wlgnE2.xls)
failure_modes: asset URL has a random suffix regenerated each release; the landing page must be scraped every month to find the current link
no stable 'latest' alias for the series
pass_criteria: stable series identifier with a 'latest' alias that resolves to the current versioned asset (keep cache-busting on the file)
DCAT catalogue record
cases.probe: Resolve the series 'latest' alias twice across releases; assert the same identifier resolves to the current data without scraping a human page.

cases.title: POSITIVE CONTROL — ONS time series via CDID (e.g. CPIH/inflation)
pass_criteria: stable code, clean JSON endpoint, typed history — already met
to reach M4: in-band unit/coverage/revision metadata and a catalogue record
cases.probe: Resolve the CDID; GET JSON; assert a typed time series. Positive control: the gold standard already inside government — proves the rest is achievable.

cases.title: POSITIVE CONTROL — World Bank indicator (aggregator)
cases.domain: World Bank (aggregates national outputs, often ONS)
failure_modes: downstream aggregator: timely-but-stale and methodologically smoothed
usability is borrowed — the harmonisation work was done off the primary publisher's books
pass_criteria: clean uniform endpoint across indicators and countries — already met
lesson: a domestic harmonisation layer (National Data Library) should do this for UK primary outputs
cases.probe: Resolve an indicator; GET JSON; assert a uniform typed series. Positive control for cross-country comparability and for what a domestic harmonisation layer should provide.
